data.title: U.S. President Trump Proposes Ending Seniority Protections in Federal Layoffs
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has proposed a significant change to the federal workforce policy by suggesting the removal of seniority protections during layoffs. According to Jin10, this move aims to streamline the process and potentially prioritize performance over tenure. The proposal has sparked discussions among policymakers and federal employees, as it could alter the dynamics of job security within government positions. Critics argue that removing seniority protections might lead to increased uncertainty for long-serving employees, while supporters believe it could enhance efficiency and meritocracy in the federal workforce. The debate continues as stakeholders assess the potential impacts of this policy shift.

data.title: Ford's February U.S. Auto Production and Electric Vehicle Sales Figures Released
data.description: Ford Motor Company reported its U.S. auto production for February, reaching a total of 188,197 vehicles. According to Jin10, the company also announced that its electric vehicle sales in the U.S. for the same month totaled 14,132 units. This data highlights Ford's ongoing efforts to expand its presence in the electric vehicle market while maintaining robust overall production numbers.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin shows signs of recovery after five consecutive days of decline; healthy expansion of derivatives points to a phase of recovery.
data.description: A Bitfinex report points out that Bitcoin has experienced five consecutive months of decline since October 2025, marking the first time since 2018 that it has formed a "five-month losing streak." February alone saw a 14.93% drop, with a cumulative maximum drawdown of approximately 52.34%. However, early signs of recovery emerged in March. Data shows that since March 1st, approximately $3.2 billion worth of BTC has been systematically bought at market prices across exchanges, successfully recovering the $65,000 mark. The Coinbase premium index ended 40 consecutive days of negative values ​​and turned positive, indicating a return of spot buying from the US. The derivatives structure also remains relatively healthy: open interest rose to $53.1 billion, a 15.4% increase from Sunday's close, but the perpetual funding rate was only about 9.5% APR, showing no signs of overheating. OI (Online Inflow) and spot trading expanded in tandem, reflecting that this round of gains was largely driven by spot market absorption. Regarding ETFs, the US spot Bitcoin ETF recorded a net inflow of approximately $1.1 billion last week, with over $450 million flowing in on Monday and Tuesday combined, indicating that institutional demand remains the core support. Analysts believe that if key support levels hold, Bitcoin may recover to the $80,000-$85,000 range within the next 1-3 months; in the short term, attention should be paid to the $72,000-$74,000 area of ​​concentrated short selling and the potential dynamic support level of $66,000. The overall assessment remains cautiously bullish.

data.title: An insider wallet, suspected of being linked to the US-Iran conflict, invested $3,000 to predict the Iranian regime would collapse before March 31.
data.description: According to Lookonchain's monitoring, an insider wallet suspected of being linked to the US-Iran conflict, after three days of inactivity, invested $3,000 to predict that the Iranian regime would collapse before March 31. The wallet was created on February 28 and had only made two prediction trades before, both of which were successful, with a profit of $16,000.

data.title: The UK House of Lords questioned Coinbase executives about the risks of stablecoins.
data.description: The UK House of Lords questioned Tom Duff Gordon, Coinbase's Vice President of International Policy, on Wednesday, focusing on whether stablecoins could lead to a loss of bank deposits and pose new risks to the UK financial system. Gordon stated that fully reserve and regulated stablecoins are "safer than uninsured bank deposits" because they are backed 1:1 by cash and high-quality government securities and are redeemable at face value. He emphasized that stablecoins can significantly reduce payment costs and accelerate cross-border payments. Members of the House of Lords questioned the redemption risks of stablecoins during a crisis, their potential impact on banks, and their role in criminal activities. Gordon countered that concerns about bank disintermediation were "grossly exaggerated" and stressed that Coinbase strictly enforces KYC and anti-money laundering measures. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Study Highlights Impact of Aid Cuts on Child Marriage Progress
data.description: A recent study, supported by former Meta executive Sheryl Sandberg, reveals that reductions in humanitarian and development aid may hinder or reverse advancements in ending child marriages. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential consequences of these funding cuts on global efforts to combat child marriage. The study emphasizes the importance of sustained financial support to maintain progress in this area. Without adequate resources, initiatives aimed at preventing child marriages could face significant setbacks, affecting millions of young girls worldwide. The findings underscore the critical role of international aid in addressing this pressing issue.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Mary Daly on Inflation and Labor Market Outlook
data.description: Federal Reserve Board member Mary Daly has expressed that the market does not seem concerned about long-term inflation expectations. According to NS3.AI, Daly also noted that it is premature to confirm any weakening trend in the labor market. Her comments suggest ongoing vigilance but no immediate alarm regarding inflation or labor market conditions.

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Advocates for Interest Rate Cut
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Milan has suggested that a one percent interest rate reduction this year would be suitable. According to NS3.AI, he argues that the current rates are approximately one percent above the neutral rate, indicating a possible shift towards a more accommodative monetary policy soon.

data.title: Federal Reserve Plans Continued Rate Cuts Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Milian has announced that the central bank intends to proceed with interest rate cuts at the upcoming March meeting. According to NS3.AI, despite the ongoing conflict in Iran, the Federal Reserve's outlook remains steady. Milian emphasized the goal of reducing rates by 0.25% increments until a neutral interest rate level is achieved, at which point the situation will be reassessed.

data.title: Market news: The EU is expected to be exempted from the US tariffs, which have generally increased to 15%.
data.description: According to market sources, the EU is expected to be exempted from the tariffs that the US has generally increased to 15%. (Jinshi)

data.title: A whale closed its short position in ETH, incurring a loss of over $1.529 million, and withdrew all funds from its wallet.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale has closed its short position in ETH. It is reported that the position was opened within 24 hours, and the loss after closing the position exceeded $1.529 million. All funds in the wallet have now been withdrawn.

data.title: Sui's native stablecoin, Sui Dollar (USDsui), has been officially launched.
data.description: Sui Dollar (USDsui), the native stablecoin of the Sui blockchain, launched today. Issued by Bridge, a stablecoin company acquired by payment giant Stripe last year, the yield generated by its underlying assets will be returned to the Sui ecosystem. Adeniyi Abiodun, co-founder of Mysten Labs, stated that the yield generated from bonds and liquid assets backing USDsui will be used to buy back and burn SUI tokens, or deployed to decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols and automated market makers to incentivize trading. This contrasts with the model of mainstream stablecoin issuers like Tether and Circle, which retain all yield. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Significant Bitcoin Transfer to Cumberland DRW Identified
data.description: A substantial Bitcoin transaction has been detected, involving the transfer of 54.999894 BTC. According to ChainCatcher, this transfer occurred at 21:39 from an anonymous address beginning with 168Bve to Cumberland DRW. The movement of such a significant amount of Bitcoin highlights ongoing activity within the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Justin Sun Faces Continued Social Media Bans in China
data.description: Justin Sun, the creator of TRON and a well-known figure in the cryptocurrency world, has experienced another social media setback with the banning of his account on Rednote, a platform similar to Instagram in China. According to NS3.AI, this ban is part of a series of actions by Chinese social media platforms against Sun, who has previously faced bans on TikTok and several Weibo accounts. Despite his significant following, Sun now finds himself without an active presence on Chinese social media. This situation highlights the stricter policing of figures like Sun on Chinese platforms compared to Western social media, where he continues to have millions of followers.

data.title: Polish Zloty Rises Slightly Against Euro Following Central Bank Rate Cut
data.description: The Polish zloty experienced a modest increase against the euro after the country's central bank implemented an interest rate cut. According to Jin10, this monetary policy adjustment aims to stimulate economic growth amid ongoing financial challenges. The central bank's decision reflects a broader trend among global financial institutions to adopt accommodative measures in response to economic uncertainties. Market analysts are closely monitoring the impact of this rate cut on Poland's economic outlook and currency stability.

data.title: EU Expected to Be Exempt from U.S. Tariff Increase
data.description: The European Union is anticipated to be exempt from the United States' general tariff increase to 15%, according to Jin10. This development comes amid ongoing trade discussions and negotiations between the two economic powers. The exemption is seen as a positive step in maintaining stable trade relations and avoiding potential economic disruptions. The decision reflects the importance of the EU-U.S. trade partnership and the efforts to foster cooperation in the face of global economic challenges.

data.title: GSR withdrew 3,000 ETH from Binance 3 hours ago, worth $6.23 million.
data.description: According to The Data Nerd, GSR withdrew 3,000 ETH (approximately $6.23 million) from Binance within 3 hours.

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Milan: It is appropriate to continue cutting interest rates at the March meeting; the outlook remains unchanged despite the outbreak of the conflict with Iran.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Milan stated that it would be appropriate to continue cutting interest rates at the March meeting; the outlook remains unchanged despite the outbreak of the conflict in Iran, and he hopes to continue cutting rates by 0.25 percentage points each time until the Fed's interest rates reach a neutral level, at which point a reassessment will be conducted. (Jinshi)

data.title: Sri Lankan Deputy Foreign Minister Comments on Ship's Route
data.description: Sri Lankan Deputy Foreign Minister stated that the ship was returning to Iran from an eastern Indian port. According to Jin10, this information was disclosed during a recent briefing. The ship's journey has attracted attention due to its geopolitical implications, as it navigates through a region with complex international relations. The vessel's route highlights ongoing maritime activities between India and Iran, reflecting broader regional dynamics. Further details about the ship's cargo or specific purpose were not provided in the statement.

data.title: Poll Shows Growing Russian Support for Peace Talks Amid Ukraine Conflict
data.description: A recent opinion poll indicates that a record number of Russians are in favor of peace negotiations over the continuation of the Kremlin's military actions in Ukraine. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that despite the increasing preference for diplomatic solutions, support for the military operation remains substantial among the Russian populace. The survey reflects a shift in public sentiment as the conflict persists, with many citizens advocating for a resolution through dialogue rather than prolonged warfare. This development comes amid ongoing international discussions and efforts to address the situation in Ukraine.
